project_type: Next.js 16 monolithic app with Supabase PostgreSQL backend
build_system: pnpm with Next.js built-in build (no Makefile). Uses `pnpm build` for production builds, `pnpm lint` for linting.
test_framework: No test framework configured yet. No test files exist.
verification_instructions: 1. Run `pnpm install` to ensure deps. 2. Run `pnpm lint` for lint checks. 3. Run `pnpm build` to confirm TypeScript compilation and Next.js build succeeds.
setup_instructions: 1. cd /projects/sandbox/inventaris-paud. 2. pnpm install. 3. cp .env.example .env.local (fill Supabase values). 4. pnpm dev to start.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode - full access. Node.js v22 with pnpm available. No Docker needed. No test framework exists yet.
contribution_requirements: Indonesian language for all UI text. Mobile-first design with large touch targets. Tailwind CSS 4 for styling. TypeScript strict mode. @/ path alias for src/.
key_patterns: Single page.tsx monolith with all state/UI in one file. Server-side Supabase via getSupabaseServerClient() in API routes. Client-side API calls via src/lib/api-client.ts. inventory-store.ts is the server-side data layer. Zones are identified by slug. Items reference zone_id (UUID) in DB but use slug as zoneId in app. No routing beyond / and /api/* currently.
directory_structure: src/app/ - Next.js app router pages and API routes. src/lib/ - shared utilities (supabase client, inventory types/store, api-client, media, reporting). supabase/ - schema.sql and migrations/. public/ - static assets. docs/ - project documentation.
